metadata.title: West Windsor Township 2026 Adopted Budget
extraction_policy: Values are copied from the supplied workbook without recalculation.
Blank workbook cells are represented as null, not zero.
Original labels, FCOA codes, notes, and source-sheet references are retained.

overview: WEST WINDSOR TOWNSHIP — 2026 ADOPTED BUDGET (complete dataset)
Township of West Windsor, County of Mercer, NJ · Fiscal Year 2026 · Adopted Copy
PLAIN-ENGLISH SNAPSHOT (all figures taken directly from the budget; sheet noted in [ ])
Total 2026 budget (Total General Appropriations = Total General Revenues): $57,685,649.81  [Sheets 3, 11, 29]
How it is paid for:
- Property taxes for municipal purposes: $28,550,275.18  [Sheet 11, line 6a]
- Surplus (prior-year fund balance) used: $13,425,000.00  [Sheet 11, line 1]
- Other miscellaneous revenues: $15,210,374.63  [Sheet 11, line 3]
- Receipts from delinquent taxes: $500,000.00  [Sheet 11, line 4]
How it is spent (appropriations):
- Within the state spending cap ('CAPS'): $42,399,438.79  [Sheet 19, H-1]
- Excluded from the cap (debt, capital, shared services, grants, etc.): $13,083,413.13  [Sheet 28, H-2]
- Reserve for uncollected taxes: $2,202,797.89  [Sheet 29, M]
Surplus: fund balance was $21,750,611.01 on 12/31/2025; $13,425,000.00 used here, leaving $8,325,611.01.  [Sheet 39]
BUDGET PROCESS DATES (Sheets 1 & 2)
Approved by Township Council resolution: April 13, 2026.  Published: April 14, 2026.
Public hearing: May 11, 2026, 7:00 PM, 271 Clarksville Road. Reserve for uncollected taxes based on est. 98.95% collection.
TAB INDEX
Glossary | Officials | Budget Summary | 2025 Approps Expended | CAP Calculation | Group Insurance Recap
Levy CAP Banks | Levy CAP Calculation | Revenues | Revenues Summary | Appropriations | Appropriations Summary
Capital Budget 2026 | 6-Year Capital Program | Capital Funding Sources | Open Space Trust Fund | Arts & Culture Trust
Dedicated Assessment | Fund Balance & Surplus | Change Orders
FIDELITY / NO-HALLUCINATION RULES
1. Every figure is transcribed directly from the named budget sheet; no outside numbers were added.
2. Printed totals are shown exactly as printed; nothing was recomputed or 'corrected'.
3. A blank amount cell = the line is blank or '-' on the form (no amount). No '0' was invented.
4. Negative values appear in parentheses, matching the form's '( )' notation.
5. The 'Source (Sheet)' column tells you exactly where to verify a value.
6. The source is a SCANNED PDF (no digital text layer). Where a digit was unclear it was reconciled to the row/column
printed total, and any such cell is flagged in its Notes column.
7. Functional groups & plain-language labels follow the document's own department structure and change no figure.
KNOWN SOURCE QUIRK (flagged, not changed)
Sheet 25 'Operations Excluded from CAPS' detail: S&W 158,662.00 + Other Expenses 6,731,589.13 = 6,890,251.13,
which is $3,500.00 below the printed total 6,893,751.13. That $3,500.00 is the 'Matching Funds for Grants' line,
which the form counts in the total but not in the S&W/OE split.

glossary.title: Glossary of Budget Terms
glossary.description: Plain-English definitions of standard New Jersey municipal-budget terms.

rows.plain_english_meaning: Money budgeted to spend on a specific purpose during the year.
Money the township expects to receive during the year to fund the budget.
A NJ limit on how much most municipal spending can grow year to year.
A separate NJ limit on how much the amount raised by property taxes can grow (~2%, with allowed exclusions).
Whether a line counts against the appropriation cap. Debt, capital, shared services and grants are usually 'excluded'.
Leftover money from prior years; a portion is 'anticipated' (used) each year to help fund the budget.
Money budgeted to cover the share of taxes historically not collected, keeping the township cash-solvent.
The part of a line that pays employees.
Non-salary operating costs (supplies, services, contracts, utilities).
Amount actually spent or obligated against last year's budget.
Budgeted money not spent last year and set aside.
Last year's budget after mid-year transfers between lines.
'Functional Chart of Accounts' code — NJ's standard account number for each line.
Mandatory payments required by law, e.g., pension contributions and Social Security.
Public Employees' / Police and Firemen's Retirement System (state pensions).
Defined Contribution Retirement Program.
Payments of principal and interest on bonds and notes.
Short-term borrowing used until bonds are issued.
A reserve providing the local 'down payment' share of capital projects.
Capital Budget = this year's projects; Capital Program = the 6-year plan.
Outside funding (state/federal/county/private) for specific programs, offset by appropriations.
Services shared with, provided to, or received from another government.
Money that by law can only be used for a specific purpose (open space, recycling, donations).
Voter-approved dedicated tax for buying/maintaining open space, recreation, farmland and historic sites.
Added assessed value of new construction, which expands the tax base.
The municipal property-tax rate, in dollars per $100 of assessed value.

(Transcribed verbatim from Sheet 38.)

- Excluded from the cap (debt, capital, shared services, grants, etc.): $13,083,413.13  [Sheet 28, H-2]
Surplus: fund balance was $21,750,611.01 on 12/31/2025; $13,425,000.00 used here, leaving $8,325,611.01.  [Sheet 39]
Approved by Township Council resolution: April 13, 2026.  Published: April 14, 2026.

raw_sheets.name: Glossary
cells: Plain-English definitions of standard New Jersey municipal-budget terms.
Money budgeted to spend on a specific purpose during the year.
Money the township expects to receive during the year to fund the budget.
A NJ limit on how much most municipal spending can grow year to year.
A separate NJ limit on how much the amount raised by property taxes can grow (~2%, with allowed exclusions).
Whether a line counts against the appropriation cap. Debt, capital, shared services and grants are usually 'excluded'.
Leftover money from prior years; a portion is 'anticipated' (used) each year to help fund the budget.
Money budgeted to cover the share of taxes historically not collected, keeping the township cash-solvent.
The part of a line that pays employees.
Non-salary operating costs (supplies, services, contracts, utilities).
Amount actually spent or obligated against last year's budget.
Budgeted money not spent last year and set aside.
Last year's budget after mid-year transfers between lines.
'Functional Chart of Accounts' code — NJ's standard account number for each line.
Mandatory payments required by law, e.g., pension contributions and Social Security.
Public Employees' / Police and Firemen's Retirement System (state pensions).
Payments of principal and interest on bonds and notes.
Short-term borrowing used until bonds are issued.
A reserve providing the local 'down payment' share of capital projects.
Capital Budget = this year's projects; Capital Program = the 6-year plan.
Outside funding (state/federal/county/private) for specific programs, offset by appropriations.
Services shared with, provided to, or received from another government.
Money that by law can only be used for a specific purpose (open space, recycling, donations).
Voter-approved dedicated tax for buying/maintaining open space, recreation, farmland and historic sites.
Added assessed value of new construction, which expands the tax base.
The municipal property-tax rate, in dollars per $100 of assessed value.
